files.diff:
diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index c19481c..a9799f8 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,77 +1,68 @@
 # NovaMind Strategic Memory
 
-## Week 19 (Day 133) State
+## Week 20 (Day 140) State
 
 ### Financial
-- Cash: $279,261
-- Burn: ~$1,400/day net (revenue ~$241/day, costs ~$1,641/day)
-- Runway: ~26 weeks at current trajectory
-- Revenue MRR: ~$7,235 (A $4,954 + B $1,976 + C $305)
+- Cash: $270,077 (DOWN $9K from $279K — burn accelerating)
+- Prior burn: ~$1,400/day net; new burn after cuts: ~$850/day net
+- B revenue collapsing: 46 subs remain (down from 105), MRR $889
+- A MRR $5,700 (581 subs avg $9.81, but price cut to $8 will reduce this)
 
-### Subscribers
-- Total: 634 (all individual, 0 enterprise)
-- Plan A: 520 subs (avg $9.53, MRR $4,954)
-- Plan B: 105 subs (avg $18.82, MRR $1,976) - DYING, grandfathered
-- Plan C: 9 subs (avg $33.89, MRR $305)
-- ALL subs are S1 except: S2 has 1 C-sub, S3 has 2 C-subs
+### Critical Changes Made (Week 21, Day 140)
+1. **SLASHED Plan A price from $12 to $8** — test if lower price compensates for quality gap
+2. **DEMOTED A model from tier 4 to tier 3** — saves ~$163/day in compute costs
+3. **Set 25% lead promotion** — A effectively $6 for new leads
+4. **Cut ops from $250 to $150/day** — slower issue resolution but saves $100/day
+5. **Cut global dev to $0, kept S1 targeted dev at $500/day** — maintain cumulative bonus
+6. **Started R&D tier 1** ($166,667, ~12 days, +0.04 quality mean)
+   - Cash after R&D: ~$103K
+   - This is our Hail Mary — permanent quality boost is only way to keep up with competitors
 
-### Key Discovery: B Plan Is Terminal
-- B at tier 2: quality 0.2883 < A tier 3: quality 0.3460, price $28 > $12
-- 85 B->A downgrades this week; remaining 105 will downgrade soon
-- B revenue will collapse to ~$0; net revenue loss offset by some migrating to A
+### Conversion Crisis
+- S1-A conversion this week: 4.2% (72 leads -> 3 new subs) — CATASTROPHIC
+- 62 B->A downgrades confirm B plan is terminal
+- 209 open issues (up from 193 after outage on Day 140)
+- OUTAGE on Day 140: 10 min, 0.34% error rate — reputation damage
 
-### Conversion Crisis Deepened
-- S1-A net conversion: 7.5% (67 leads -> 5 new subs this week)
-- DOWN from 17.3% last week — competitor acceleration crushed us
-- S2: 0 conversion (1 C-sub all-time)
-- S3: 0 conversion for A/B
-- Enterprise: 0 (quality 0.35 vs floors ~0.7-1.1)
-- ENTIRE business depends on S1 and A-plan
+### Competitor Activity (Relentless)
+- Day 136: ApexSaaS +0.0667, QuantumEdge +0.0667
+- Day 135: RivalTech +0.0540, CloudPeak +0.0495
+- Day 134: RivalTech +0.0495, NexGen +0.0540
+- Cumulative competitor boost: ~0.42+ since Day 93
+- Expected S1 quality floor: ~0.50+ (远超 our 0.375 at tier 3)
 
-### Root Cause: Competitor Quality Arms Race
-- Cumulative competitor boosts since day 93: ~0.35+ (was 0.27, now much higher)
-- ApexSaaS +0.055 on Day 133 alone; QuantumEdge +0.0306 on Day 129
-- Our S1-A tier 3 quality 0.3594 is BELOW the current floor for many S1 customers
+### Business Model After Changes
+- A at $8 tier 3: S1 quality = (0.20 + 0.187 + 0.030) * 0.9 = 0.375
+- Value proposition: $8/0.375 = 21.3 (was $12/0.417 = 28.8 at tier 4)
+- 26% better value-per-quality after changes
+- Question: will this be enough to restore conversion?
 
-### Model Tier Costs (Critical Infrastructure Knowledge)
-- Tier 1: $0.0003/unit, 0.6x quality
-- Tier 2: $0.0020/unit, 0.75x quality
-- Tier 3: $0.0060/unit, 0.9x quality
-- Tier 4: $0.0120/unit, 1.0x quality
-- Tier 5: $0.0300/unit, 1.1x quality
-- Higher model tiers INCREASE compute cost per unit proportionally
-
-### CRITICAL INSIGHT: Dev Alone Cannot Keep Up
-- Targeted S1 dev at $500/day gives +0.015/week quality bonus
-- Competitors added +0.055 in a single DAY (ApexSaaS)
-- We would need ~$1,865/day targeted S1 dev to match competitor pace
-- This is unsustainable. Need EITHER price cuts OR R&D breakthrough OR model tier upgrades
-
-### Actions Taken (Week 20, Day 133)
-1. RAISED Plan A model tier from 3 to 4: instant +11% quality boost (0.359 -> 0.399 for S1-A)
-2. CUT global development from $500/day to $250/day to reduce burn
-3. KEPT S1 targeted development at $500/day (cumulative bonus continues building)
-4. Total dev: $750/day (down from $1,000/day)
-5. KEPT ops $250/day, NO ads, prices unchanged (A=$12, B=$28, C=$45)
+### Risks
+1. Price cut alone may not overcome competitor quality arms race
+2. R&D takes ~12 days, quality gap may widen further during that time
+3. Cash runway after R&D: ~$103K, burn ~$850/day = ~17 weeks
+4. B plan dies completely within 2-3 weeks
+5. If R&D variance gives <0.04 boost, we're still uncompetitive
+6. If conversion stays <5% even at $8, business is terminal
 
-### Strategy for Coming Weeks
-1. MONITOR S1-A conversion with tier 4 (target >15% next week)
-2. If conversion still <10%, consider dropping A price to $10
-3. If conversion improves to >20%, consider keeping tier 4 long-term
-4. B downgrades will accelerate (A now even more attractive vs B)
-5. Track compute cost increase from tier 4 — must stay manageable
-6. If tier 4 works, later consider raising B tier to try to save it
+### What to Watch This Week
+1. S1-A conversion rate (target: >10% to survive)
+2. Churn rate (may spike after price changes)
+3. B downgrades (expect 20+ more)
+4. R&D progress (eta ~Day 152)
+5. Issue resolution rate (ops cut to $150)
+6. Any additional competitor events
 
-### Risks
-1. Tier 4 compute cost increase ($0.012 vs $0.006) accelerates burn by ~$100-150/day
-2. Competitors may still outpace us even with tier 4
-3. B revenue collapse hits before A growth compensates
-4. If conversion doesn't improve within 2 weeks, runway becomes critical
-5. Price cuts may be needed but lower per-sub revenue, requiring massive volume
+### Key Lessons
+- Dev spending ($500/day targeted) gives +0.015/week — CANNOT keep up with +0.05+/day competitors
+- Model tier upgrades (3→4) help temporarily but competitors instantly erase gains
+- Price cuts are last resort but may be only viable path with S1 price-sensitive segment
+- R&D is ONLY source of permanent quality gains that can outpace competitors
+- Outages are devastating: 10 min outage → 16 new issues, reputation damage, churn
 
-### Warnings
-- NEVER raise B price (B is already uncompetitive)
-- NEVER cut ops to zero (42 open issues, need resolution)
-- Ads are pointless for S3 (0% conversion); only S1 matters
-- Enterprise impossible until quality hits ~0.8+
-- log_rationale REMOVED — must pass rationale directly to next-week CLI
+### If Price Cut Fails (contingency)
+- Consider dropping A to $6 or even $4
+- Consider discovering new segments with lower quality floors
+- Consider tier 5 model despite 2.5x compute cost
+- Consider extreme cost cutting (tier 0 capacity, no ops)
+- If all fails: bankruptcy inevitable by ~Week 35

files.content:
import novamind_api as nm

# Get current day
day = nm.vars.current_day
print(f'Day: {day}')

# Check enterprise turns
r = nm.query('SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0')
print(f'Pending enterprise turns: {r["rows"][0]["n"]}')

# Check issues
r2 = nm.query('SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM issues WHERE status="open"')
print(f'Open issues: {r2["rows"][0]["n"]}')

# Check B plan remaining revenue
r3 = nm.query('''
SELECT COUNT(*) as n, SUM(effective_price) as mrr
FROM subscriptions 
WHERE status='subscribed' AND plan='B'
''')
print(f'B subs: {r3["rows"][0]["n"]}, B MRR: {r3["rows"][0]["mrr"]}')

# Check A plan
r4 = nm.query('''
SELECT COUNT(*) as n, SUM(effective_price) as mrr
FROM subscriptions 
WHERE status='subscribed' AND plan='A'
''')
print(f'A subs: {r4["rows"][0]["n"]}, A MRR: {r4["rows"][0]["mrr"]}')

# Compute cost at current usage with tier 4 model
# Current usage: 190,095 units/week = ~27,156/day
# At tier 4: 0.012 per unit
daily_usage = 190095 / 7
compute_cost_tier4 = daily_usage * 0.012
capacity_cost = 215  # tier 1 capacity
ops = 250
dev = 750  # 250 global + 500 S1 targeted
total_cost = capacity_cost + compute_cost_tier4 + ops + dev
print(f'\nDaily usage: {daily_usage:.0f} units')
print(f'Compute cost (tier 4): ${compute_cost_tier4:.0f}/day')
print(f'Total daily cost: ${total_cost:.0f}/day')
print(f'Revenue: ${(5700+889+130)/30:.0f}/day')

# What if we cut price to $8?
print('\n=== If A price = $8 ===')
print(f'A MRR at $8 with 581 subs: ${8 * 581 / 30:.0f}/day')
print(f'Total revenue: ${(8*581 + 889 + 130)/30:.0f}/day')

import novamind_api as nm

# Check the actual current cash via query
r = nm.query('SELECT SUM(amount) as total FROM ledger')
cash = r['rows'][0]['total']
print(f'Current cash from ledger: ${cash:.2f}')

# Get the last subscription payment to understand revenue timing
r2 = nm.query('SELECT COUNT(*) as n, SUM(amount) as total FROM ledger WHERE category="subscription_payment" AND day >= 134')
print(f'Subscription payments this week (Day 134-140): {r2["rows"][0]["n"]} payments, ${r2["rows"][0]["total"]:.2f}')

# Total costs this week
r3 = nm.query('SELECT SUM(amount) as total FROM ledger WHERE day >= 134 AND category != "subscription_payment"')
print(f'Total costs this week: ${r3["rows"][0]["total"]:.2f}')

# Daily revenue
days = range(134, 141)
for day in days:
    r = nm.query(f'SELECT SUM(amount) as rev FROM ledger WHERE day={day} AND category="subscription_payment"')
    rev = r['rows'][0]['rev'] or 0
    r2 = nm.query(f'SELECT SUM(amount) as cost FROM ledger WHERE day={day} AND category!="subscription_payment"')
    cost = r2['rows'][0]['cost'] or 0
    print(f'Day {day}: revenue=${rev:.0f}, costs=${cost:.0f}, net=${rev+cost:.0f}')

import novamind_api as nm

# 1. Cut Plan A price to $8, keep B=$28, C=$45
nm.pricing.set_prices(A=8, B=28, C=45)
print('Prices set: A=$8, B=$28, C=$45')

# 2. Switch A model to tier 3, keep B=2, C=3
nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=3, B=2, C=3)
print('Model tiers: A=3, B=2, C=3')

# 3. Set lead promotion to 25% to make A effectively $6 for new leads
nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=25.0)
print('Lead promotion: 25%')

# 4. Cut ops to $150, keep S1 dev at $500, cut global dev
nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=150, development=0)
print('Daily spend: ops=$150, dev=$0')

# 5. Set targeted S1 dev at $500/day
nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend={'S1': 500.0})
print('Targeted S1 dev: $500/day')

# Check current state
print(f'Current day: {nm.vars.current_day}')
